data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <bead id="ddx-c6e3db02">
    <title>ddx work: per-bead rate-limit retry honoring Retry-After (provider stays in rotation)</title>
    <description>
Distinct from quota exhaustion (see ddx-aede917d). Rate-limiting (HTTP 429 with Retry-After header — per-second/per-minute throttle) means 'slow down', not 'I'm out for the period'. Provider stays in rotation; current bead retries after waiting.

Per-bead retry policy:
1. On 429 / rate-limit error from Fizeau (NOT NoViableProviderForNow which is quota): wait the Retry-After interval (capped at 60s default, configurable via --rate-limit-max-wait), retry the SAME bead with the SAME provider
2. If no Retry-After header: exponential backoff (1s, 5s, 15s, 30s, 60s) up to the cap
3. Per-bead total rate-limit wait budget (default 5 min); past that, mark execution_failed with reason 'rate-limited beyond budget'
4. Provider availability state unchanged (stays in rotation; this is per-request throttle, not provider-out)

Should use Fizeau's existing RecordRouteAttempt feedback channel for transparency (records the rate-limit event so the routing engine has signal even though it doesn't change provider availability).
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. 429 errors from Fizeau trigger per-bead wait + retry per TD-NNN RateLimitRetryContract (per ddx-673833f4). 2. **Per RateLimitRetryContract: NO persisted status change before budget exhaustion; provider stays in rotation. bd/br compatibility (ADR-004) preserved.** 3. Retry-After honored when present; exponential backoff fallback when absent. 4. --rate-limit-max-wait flag bounds per-bead total wait (default 5 min). 5. Beyond budget: bead transitions per TD's outcome→state mapping for 'rate-limited beyond budget' — status stays in canonical 6, additional context conveyed via labels/events. 6. RecordRouteAttempt invoked for transparency per RateLimitRetryContract. 7. Tests cover Retry-After-respected and exponential-backoff paths. 8. Quota-exhaustion path is explicitly NOT this bead's concern (per QuotaPauseContract separation in TD). 9. **schema_compat_test.go round-trip passes after this bead lands.**
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:2, story:10, area:agent, kind:feature</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-e34994e2" base-rev="fd79ebd080872e25434d37d0a38cebfcd0a853a1" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260502T205533-a458a02f"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read those before editing. Ground decisions in what is already in the repository; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260502T205533-a458a02f/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260502T205533-a458a02f/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

If you write `.ddx/executions/20260502T205533-a458a02f/no_changes_rationale.txt`, it MUST carry one of:

- `verification_command: <one-line shell command>` — the loop runs it (cwd = project root, 60s); exit 0 closes the bead, non-zero rejects.
- `status: needs_investigation` + `reason: <why>` — bead stays open for triage.

Bare rationales ("nothing to do") are rejected.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260502T205533-a458a02f/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260502T205533-a458a02f/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Bead overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and conservative project defaults (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) in this worktree — write whatever the bead asks for.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
</execute-bead>

data.output: You're out of extra usage · resets 6:20pm (America/New_York)
